content.text: Answer seven problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

body.text: Let \(M/K\) be a field extension and let \(p\) be a prime. Let \(L_1/K, L_2/K\) be finite subextensions of \(M/K\) such that \([L_1:K]\) and \([L_2:K]\) are both powers of \(p\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(L_1/K\) is a Galois extension then \([L_1L_2:K]\) is a power of \(p\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example which shows that if neither of \(L_1/K, L_2/K\) is Galois then \([L_1L_2:K]\) need not be a power of \(p\).

body.text: Prove that every field \(K\) is contained in an algebraically closed field.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Let \(C\) be a category and let \(f:X\to Y\) be a morphism in \(C\). Define what it means for \(f\) to have each of the following properties:

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example of a category \(C\) and a morphism \(f:X\to Y\) in \(C\) which is a monomorphism and an epimorphism, but not an isomorphism.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what it means for a group \(G\) to be nilpotent.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(G\) is nilpotent and \(N\trianglelefteq G\) then \(G/N\) is nilpotent.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(G\) is free on a set \(S\) and \(|S|>1\) then \(G\) is not nilpotent.

body.text: Let \(0\to A\mathrel{\mathop{\to}^{f}}B\mathrel{\mathop{\to}^{g}}C\to 0\) be an exact sequence of groups (not necessarily abelian).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Suppose there is a group homomorphism \(r:B\to A\) such that \(r\circ f=\operatorname{id}_A\). Prove that \(B\cong A\times C\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Suppose there is a group homomorphism \(s:C\to B\) such that \(g\circ s=\operatorname{id}_C\). Prove that \(B\cong A\rtimes_\phi C\) for some \(\phi:C\to\operatorname{Aut}(A)\).

body.text:
Let \(R\) be a commutative ring with 1 and let \(I\) be a proper ideal of \(R\). The radical of \(I\) is defined to be
\[
\sqrt{I}=\{x\in R:x^n\in I\text{ for some }n\geq 1\}.
\]
Prove that \(\sqrt{I}\) is equal to the intersection of all the prime ideals of \(R\) which contain \(I\).
Let \(R\) be a commutative ring with 1 and let \(P\) and \(Q\) be projective \(R\)-modules. Prove that the \(R\)-module \(P\otimes_R Q\) is projective.
Let \(R\) be an integral domain, let \(K\) be field of fractions of \(R\), and let \(A\subset K\) be a fractional ideal of \(R\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that the set \(A'=\{x\in K:xA\subset R\}\) is a fractional ideal of \(R\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that the fractional ideal \(A\) is invertible if and only if \(AA'=R\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Give an example of an integral domain \(R\) and a nonzero fractional ideal \(A\) of \(R\) which is not invertible.

body.text: Let \(D>1\) be square-free. Determine the integral closure of \(\mathbb{Z}\) in \(\mathbb{Q}(\sqrt{D})\). Your answer should be carefully justified.

Let \(R\) be a ring with 1 and let \(M\) be a unital \(R\)-module. Assume that for every \(R\)-submodule \(N\) of \(M\) there is an \(R\)-submodule \(N'\) such that \(M=N\oplus N'\). Prove that if \(L\) is a nontrivial submodule of \(M\) then \(L\) contains a simple submodule.
Determine the number of isomorphism classes of noncommutative semisimple rings with \(2592=2^5\cdot 3^4\) elements. You do not have to list the rings.
